ground_truth.title: Budget Traveler's Guide To Travel Insurance
ground_truth.author: Gabby Boucher
ground_truth.main_content: Travel insurance. What's the deal? Do you really need it? How much is it? What kind should you get? I put off answering these questions for years. I've been traveling consistently since 2015 and it wasn't until 2019 that I actually bought my own travel insurance for the first time. People might be thinking I am crazy or even incredibly stupid for traveling without insurance. Honestly, they're probably right. But now I am older and wiser, and I've experienced medical issues abroad with and without having travel insurance. So I've gained a bit more insight into the world of insurance and what it actually means for us budget travelers. Insurance is when you pay money to a company so that when something specific happens, they will pay for the expenses. So TRAVEL INSURANCE is when you pay for a plan that will cover your expenses if something travel-related goes wrong, such as a canceled flight, lost baggage, trip delays, etc. Most travel insurance plans also come with emergency medical coverage. The cost of a travel insurance plan depends on where you are traveling to, how long you are traveling for, and of course, the insurance company you choose. The biggest benefit of buying travel insurance is having financial coverage and peace of mind should things go wrong. The truth is, you never know. That's the tricky thing about insurance, you never think you'll need it until suddenly you do. In my opinion, the most essential part of travel insurance is medical coverage. Airlines are so sophisticated these days that trip delays and lost bags are actually pretty rare. But medical emergencies can happen to anyone at any time. I got bit by a dog in the Transkei in South Africa. I actually got bit twice. The dog may or may not have been rabid. I needed 4 emergency rabies shots and 2 immunoglobulin shots. Everything combined, I spent close to $700 USD on my rabies treatment. I am still dumbfounded at how lucky I am that I chose THIS TRIP to buy travel insurance. Spending $60 upfront was so worth getting $700 back at the end of my trip. Travel insurance may seem unnecessary, but it's a lifesaver when you actually need it.

items.caption: I'll explain why I was grateful to have travel insurance in South Africa at the end of this article.

items.alt: girl in a village in lake atitlan travel insurance
items.caption: I always thought travel insurance was too expensive, but it can actually be affordable if you look for the right things.

items.alt: airplane flying through the colorful sky at sunrise
items.caption: Airlines usually run very smoothly, but things can still go wrong!

items.alt: elephant travel insurance for safari in Africa
items.caption: If you're getting this close to a wild bull elephant, you probably want to have travel insurance just in case.
The super sweet local herbalist in the Nquileni Village of the Transkei. This day was so amazing until I got bit by a probably rabid dog.
